about.title: USF-ADCA Platform
about.description: The reference platform for monitoring, collecting, and managing the Universal Service Fund across the African continent.

whatIs.title: What is USF-ADCA?
whatIs.p1: USF-ADCA (Universal Service Fund - African Digital Connection) is a pan-African platform developed under the auspices of the African Telecommunications Union (ATU).
whatIs.p2: It enables the 54 member countries to collect, track, and manage Universal Service Fund initiatives, ensuring better coordination and maximum impact on digital inclusion.

objectives.title: Our Objectives
objectives.description: USF-ADCA is built around four main objectives

universal.title: Universal Service
universal.desc: Ensure universal access to telecommunications services

inclusion.title: Digital Inclusion
inclusion.desc: Reduce the digital divide between urban and rural areas

monitoring.title: Monitoring and Management
monitoring.desc: Ensure effective tracking of USF projects and policies

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.desc: Facilitate exchanges between member states and partners

leadership.title: Leadership Structure
leadership.description: The leadership team of the African Telecommunications Union

secretaryGeneral.title: Secretary General
secretaryGeneral.name: Mr. John Omo
secretaryGeneral.description: Legal professional with 33 years of experience, he leads the continental body responsible for developing ICT policies and services in Africa and coordinating African participation in international forums.

mwale.name: Eng. Kezias Kazuba Mwale
mwale.description: In office since 2012. Spectrum management expert, he contributed to the allocation of 2477 MHz for broadband and the recovery of satellite orbital resources for 31 African countries.

slimani.name: Ms. Meriem Slimani
slimani.description: Joined ATU in 2015. Engineer from the National Polytechnic School of Algiers, she led e-government projects in Algeria before focusing on ATU's development mandate.

boateng.name: Mr. Isaac Boateng
boateng.description: Over 23 years of experience in ICT. Former Ghana Communications Authority official, ITU expert since 2008, he oversees standardization initiatives and represents Africa in international technical forums.

ballo.name: Mr. Djibrilla Ballo
ballo.description: In office since August 2012. Chartered accountant with multiple masters in finance and economics, he oversees the Union's financial management, strategic planning, and internal control.

partners.title: Our Partners
partners.description: We collaborate with key institutions across the African continent

history.title: Project History
history.phase1Desc: ATU initiative to create a harmonized USF monitoring platform.
history.phase2Desc: Platform design and development with pilot countries.
history.phase3Desc: Progressive deployment in the 54 ATU member countries.

hero.title: SUTEL
hero.subtitle: Universal Telecommunications Service
hero.description: Guaranteeing all citizens access to electronic communication services, for digital inclusion and reducing the digital divide.

definition.title: Definition and Regulatory Framework
definition.p1: The Universal Telecommunications Service (SUTEL) is a mechanism aimed at guaranteeing all citizens access to basic electronic communication services, regardless of their geographic location or socio-economic situation.
definition.p2: It is part of a digital inclusion and digital divide reduction approach, particularly in rural and underserved areas. SUTEL is governed by national and regional texts, often inspired by international directives and adapted to the African context.

funding.title: Funding
funding.description: Contributions from telecom operators (percentage of revenue)

management.title: Management

objectiveCard.title: Objective

event.description: Regional workshop bringing together representatives from several African countries to strengthen collaboration and share best practices. Memorandum of understanding signed between ATU and ANSUT to promote universal service as a driver of digital transformation.

hero.title: ATU Strategies and Policies
hero.description: Discover the strategic orientations and policies of the African Telecommunications Union for digital development in Africa.

intro.title: Policies for digital transformation
intro.description: The ATU develops strategies and policy frameworks to guide the development of telecommunications and digital technology across the African continent, fostering digital inclusion and universal connectivity.
intro.highlight: A commitment to a digitally connected Africa
intro.vision: Vision: A fully connected and inclusive African continent
intro.beneficiaries: Beneficiaries: Over one billion African citizens

section.title: Strategic Axes
section.description: The key strategic orientations of the ATU for the continent's digital transformation.

digitalTransformation.title: Digital Transformation
digitalTransformation.desc: Accelerate the digital transformation of member states through proactive policies and action plans adapted to African realities.

connectivity.title: Universal Connectivity
connectivity.desc: Deploy the necessary infrastructure to provide universal access to telecommunications and Internet services across the continent.

regulatoryFramework.title: Harmonized Regulatory Framework
regulatoryFramework.desc: Harmonize telecommunications legal and regulatory frameworks to facilitate investments and the development of the African single market.

inclusion.title: Digital Inclusion
inclusion.desc: Ensure equitable access to digital technologies for all citizens, particularly rural populations and vulnerable groups.

economicGrowth.title: Economic Growth
economicGrowth.desc: Leverage digital technologies as a driver of economic growth and job creation for African youth and women.

innovation.title: Innovation and Research
innovation.desc: Encourage innovation and research in telecommunications to develop solutions adapted to the continent's needs.

ourHistory.title: Our History
ourHistory.description: Discover the history and evolution of ATU-UAT through the years

hero.title: African Telecommunications Union
hero.subtitle: The evolution of ATU-UAT
hero.description: Brief history and mission of ATU-UAT

header.title: African Telecommunications Union
header.description: Continental organization promoting the development of ICT infrastructure and services in Africa

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: To promote the rapid development of information and communication technologies in Africa in order to guarantee universal access and full inter-state connectivity.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: A fully integrated Africa active in the global information and knowledge society.

briefHistory.1977: Founded in 1977 as a specialized agency of the Organization of African Unity (now African Union) in the field of telecommunications.
briefHistory.1999: In 1999, the African Telecommunications Union (ATU-UAT) takes its current name and transforms into a partnership between public and private actors in the ICT sector.
briefHistory.title: Brief History
briefHistory.today: Today, ATU-UAT provides ICT actors with a space for exchange to develop effective policies and strategies, represents the interests of its members at international conferences, and promotes initiatives to integrate regional markets, attract investment, and strengthen institutional and human capacities.

ticExchange.title: ICT Exchange Space
ticExchange.description: Development of effective policies and strategies to improve access to infrastructure

internationalRepresentation.title: International Representation
internationalRepresentation.description: Representation of members' interests at international conferences

sustainableDevelopment.title: Sustainable Development
sustainableDevelopment.description: Integration of regional markets and investment in ICT infrastructure

timelineSection.title: Key Moments
timelineSection.description: The evolution of ATU-UAT through the years

1977.title: ATU Foundation
1977.description: Created as a specialized agency of the Organization of African Unity (now African Union) in the field of telecommunications.

1999.title: Transformation to ATU-UAT
1999.description: The agency takes its current name and transforms into a partnership between public and private actors in the ICT sector.

today.title: 52 Member States
today.description: ATU currently has 52 member states and 53 associate members (fixed and mobile telecommunications operators).

organizationalStructure.title: Organizational Structure
organizationalStructure.description: The ATU-UAT leadership team is composed of the Secretary General and four regional directors covering West Africa, Central Africa, East Africa and Southern Africa. Together, they work to promote telecommunications development and ensure universal access to ICT services across the African continent.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: How we protect your personal data

intro.title: Privacy Commitment
intro.desc: The African Telecommunications Union (ATU/UAT) is committed to protecting the privacy and personal data of its users. This privacy policy describes our practices for collecting, using, and protecting information when you use our USF-ADCA platform.

collection.title: Data Collection
collection.desc: We collect information you provide directly when creating your account, submitting FSU forms, or communicating with our team. This includes your name, email address, phone number, country of origin, and professional data. We also automatically collect certain technical information during your browsing, such as IP address, browser type, and pages visited.

usage.title: Data Usage
usage.desc: Your personal data is used to: provide and improve our services, process FSU submissions and validations, communicate relevant information about ATU/UAT activities, ensure platform security, produce aggregated and anonymized statistical reports on Universal Service in Africa.

security.title: Data Security
security.desc: We implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures to protect your data against unauthorized access, modification, disclosure, or destruction. This includes data encryption in transit and at rest, two-factor authentication (2FA), role-based access controls, and regular security audits.

sharing.title: Data Sharing
sharing.desc: We only share your personal data with the following entities: national administrators of your country as part of FSU monitoring, ATU/UAT institutional partners for joint projects (under confidentiality agreements), technical service providers necessary for platform operation. No personal data is sold to third parties.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.desc: In accordance with applicable data protection legislation, you have the following rights: right of access to your personal data, right to rectification of inaccurate information, right to erasure of your data, right to data portability, right to object to the processing of your data. To exercise these rights, please contact us at the address below.

cookies.title: Cookies and Similar Technologies
cookies.desc: Our platform uses cookies and similar technologies to improve your browsing experience, remember your language preferences, analyze platform usage for improvement purposes. You can configure your browser to refuse cookies, however some features may not be available.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.desc: For any questions regarding this privacy policy or to exercise your rights, you can contact us:

legal.title: Legal Notice
legal.description: Legal information regarding the USF-ADCA platform

publisher.title: Website Publisher
publisher.desc:
The USF-ADCA website is published by the African Telecommunications Union (ATU/UAT), an international organization bringing together the telecommunications administrations of African states.

Headquarters: Nairobi, Kenya
Legal status: Intergovernmental organization

director.title: Publication Director
director.desc: The Publication Director is the Secretary General of the African Telecommunications Union (ATU/UAT).

hosting.title: Hosting
hosting.desc: The USF-ADCA platform is hosted on secure cloud infrastructure ensuring availability, redundancy, and data protection in accordance with international standards.

intellectual.title: Intellectual Property
intellectual.desc:
All content on the USF-ADCA platform (texts, images, graphics, logos, icons, software) is the exclusive property of ATU/UAT or its partners and is protected by international intellectual property laws.

Any reproduction, representation, modification, publication, or adaptation of all or part of the site's elements is prohibited without prior written authorization from ATU/UAT.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.desc: ATU/UAT strives to provide information as accurate as possible. However, it cannot be held responsible for omissions, inaccuracies, or deficiencies in the updating of information. ATU/UAT disclaims all liability for direct or indirect damages resulting from the use of the platform.

applicableLaw.title: Applicable Law
applicableLaw.desc: These legal notices are governed by international law and the founding treaties of the African Telecommunications Union. In the event of a dispute, the parties shall endeavor to find an amicable solution before any legal proceedings.

contact.title: Contact
contact.desc: For any questions regarding these legal notices, you can contact us:

hero.title: Connecting Africa
hero.subtitle: to the Digital World
hero.description: Universal Service monitoring and steering platform — Digital access for all African citizens.

welcome.title: Welcome to USF-ADCA
welcome.description: A pan-African platform dedicated to digital inclusion and telecommunications infrastructure development.

secretaryGeneral.name: Mr. Omo
secretaryGeneral.message: Dear partners and colleagues, it is with great pleasure that I welcome you to the USF-ADCA platform, a major strategic tool for Africa's digital future. This platform embodies our shared vision: ensuring universal access to telecommunications services for every African citizen, wherever they are. Together, we are building a connected, inclusive, and resilient continent. I invite you to explore this platform and contribute actively to our shared mission of digital transformation.

director.name: Mr. Gilles Beugré
director.message: Welcome to the USF-ADCA platform, the fruit of exemplary collaborative work between our member states and our technical partners. As director in charge of radiocommunications, I am convinced that optimal spectrum management and infrastructure deployment are keys to achieving our universal connectivity goals. This platform allows us to monitor, coordinate, and accelerate Universal Service Fund projects on a pan-African scale. Your commitment is essential to succeeding in this challenge.

about.title: Our Mission
about.description: To promote universal access to digital services for every African citizen, by supporting local and regional initiatives.

universal.title: Universal Access
universal.desc: Ensure that even the most remote areas benefit from quality connectivity.

dashboard.desc: A comprehensive dashboard for monitoring performance indicators at a continental scale.
